<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I have prepared a clear explanation of what a stub means in this context.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

In this project, **“stub”** means a **simplified placeholder** for a real external system — not a full integration with the Belgian Immigration Office (IBZ/DVZ).

## What it stands in for

In real municipal registration, the **federal Immigration Office** issues residence decisions. The municipality often only **records** that decision (reference number, date, outcome) as evidence on the file — it does not make the decision itself.

This app models that workflow in a minimal way for learning/demo purposes.

## What the stub actually does

Instead of calling a federal API or validating against official records, the app lets an officer **manually enter**:

- a **reference number**
- a **decision date**

Those are stored on the registration case as `ImmigrationDecisionReference`:

```3:22:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Immigration/ImmigrationDecisionReference.cs
public sealed class ImmigrationDecisionReference
{
    // ...
    public string ReferenceNumber { get; private set; } = string.Empty;
    public DateOnly DecisionDate { get; private set; }
    // ...
}
```

There is no:

- lookup against the Immigration Office
- fetch of decision details (approved/denied, permit type, etc.)
- verification that the reference number is real

The docs describe it explicitly as storing “a reference to an **external** federal immigration office decision” — the external part is assumed, not integrated.

## Why “optional for most categories”

The residence **policies** in the codebase do not require an immigration decision today:

- **EU citizen** — always passes (no permit or decision needed)
- **Non-EU worker** — checks permit type (A/B card) and expiry
- **Student** — checks permit type (Annex 15 / B card) and expiry

None of them inspect `ImmigrationDecision`. The docs note: *“stub policies do not require a decision today.”*

So the UI info box is telling officers: *you can record this if relevant, but for most demo categories it won’t block progress on legal residence.*

## How this fits the project’s “stub” pattern

The same word is used elsewhere for other external systems:

| Real system | In this project |
|-------------|-----------------|
| National Register | In-memory/DB search stub |
| Immigration Office | Manual reference entry (this feature) |
| Tax / social security notifications | Log-only stubs |

The roadmap even mentions a future **“Immigration Office stub API”** — still a **simulated** service, not production IBZ/DVZ integration.

**In short:** “stub” here means *“we model the slot in the workflow where a federal decision would go, but we fake the integration by letting the officer type a reference instead of connecting to the real Immigration Office.”*
